text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on, mega-cap tech squeeze, energy smoked. SPY gained 1.76% while QQQ ripped 3.14% and SOXX exploded 5.40% as lower yields, collapsing oil, and Iran-deal optimism drove a concentrated chase into AI and semiconductor leadership.[1][2] Positioning skewed to large-cap growth with STYLE showing LARGE > SMALL by 3.14pp as traders bought upside in mega-cap tech while fading cyclicals and small caps.[1] Cross-asset confirmed a relief rally, with USO down 3.36%, VIXY down 6.83%, and gold and silver sharply higher, underscoring a macro reset around lower energy risk and easing war premium.[1]

INDICES — VIX printed 16.2, below its 20-day moving average of 17.4, signaling cooling equity vol.[1] Today’s performance ranked: SOXX +5.40% > QQQ +3.14% > SPY +1.76% > DIA +1.05% > IWM +0.00%, with STYLE reading “LARGE > SMALL by 3.14pp,” and the standout cross-asset move was USO -3.36% as oil sold off on Iran-deal headlines.[1] Neither SPY nor QQQ is flagged below their 200-day moving averages in the provided data, so no CTA 200-day sell trigger is indicated.[1]

VOLUME — SPY traded at 1.4x average volume and QQQ at 1.2x while both advanced, signaling institutional accumulation into the rally.[1] Volume breadth showed GLD at 1.4x and XLB at 1.2x drawing attention on the buy side, while SOXX at 0.7x and XLK at 0.8x suggested the explosive tech and chip price action came on less-than-frenzied turnover, typical of a momentum-driven extension rather than a full-blown volume blowoff.[1]

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLK Technology +3.78%, XLY Discretionary +1.69%, XLI Industrials +1.42%, XLB Materials +0.61%, XLC Comms +0.48%, XLU Utilities +0.47%, XLF Financials +0.41%, XLP Staples -0.40%, XLV Healthcare -0.60%, XLRE Real Estate -0.82%, XLE Energy -3.48%.[1] Industry leaders were DRAM +9.32% and SILJ +7.10%, while laggards were OIH -3.00% and XOP -4.22%.[1] Money clearly flowed into semis and growth cyclicals, with DRAM and broader chip proxies ripping as AI infrastructure demand and lower yields supported multiple expansion.[1][2] Outflows hit energy and upstream oil and gas, with XLE -3.48% and XOP -4.22% tracking the 3.36% drop in USO as oil’s war premium deflated, making today a classic “AI hardware leading” rotation state.[1][2]

POSITIONING — SPY’s put-call volume ratio at 0.99 with ATM_IV 11.9% and positive skew of 8.5% shows balanced hedging but still notable demand for downside protection via puts.[1] QQQ and IWM PCR_vol at 1.66 and 2.07, with skew of 11.0% and 8.0% respectively, indicate traders aggressively buying protection under rich tech and still-fragile small caps while spot trades above gamma flip levels of 730.0 for QQQ and 296.0 for IWM, implying dealer hedging may dampen intraday volatility rather than amplify it.[1] VIX sitting below its 20-day average, alongside a MOVE index pinned at 0.0 in the dataset, points to equity-specific risk being priced more actively than rates volatility today.[1]

CROSS-ASSET — Oil’s 3.36% slide in USO alongside HYG +0.13%, IEF +0.11%, and GLD +2.59% created a clean “risk-on plus duration plus precious metals” alignment as easing Iran war risk pulled the energy shock premium out of the tape while simultaneously lowering macro tail-risk hedging costs.[1] Silver’s 3.56% pop in SLV despite recent 20-day weakness of -8.07% underlines a short-covering or tactical re-risking bid into metals, consistent with a broad relief rally in cyclicals and real assets following the Iran headlines.[1]

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed at 63.7/100 sits in “Greed” territory, and among notable movers Marvell Technology gained 10.23% in semis, Micron closed at $981.61 in DRAM, and Teradyne traded at $421.00 in test equipment, aligning with the DRAM +9.32% and URA +5.58% 20-day momentum extremes on the upside versus XLE -6.54% and XLC -4.09% on the downside.[1][3] KLAC’s 11.7x volume spike with a 0.74% gain in The Fab 7 basket flags quiet accumulation rather than liquidation, while FCG’s 2.5x volume with a 3.92% drop in gas equities reflects distribution out of energy Beta.[1] For tomorrow, watch KLAC (+0.74%) for continuation after the 11.7x volume signal in high-end semicap, REZ (-1.59%) for potential follow-through after a 2.6x volume break in REITs, and KCE (+1.14%) as financials credit exposure saw 4.0x volume, hinting at either early positioning for steeper curves or rotation into financial cyclicals.[1]
